sections.paragraph:
ναοφόρος, ον (ναός, φέρω; hapax leg.) pert. to bearing the image of a temple or shrine (s. ναός a), subst. temple-bearer or shrine-bearer w. other compounds of-φόρος IEph 9:2. Evidently the writer has a polytheistic relig. procession in mind.—DELG s.v. φέρω D 2 p. 1190.

hapax leg. hapax leg. = hapax legomenon (occurring only once)

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

relig. relig. = religious

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 666
